title: OpenAI Launches Codex, Its Most Capable AI Coding Agent
summary: OpenAI launched a research preview of Codex, a cloud-based AI coding agent powered by codex-1 that can write features, fix bugs, and run tests across multiple tasks simultaneously.
content: OpenAI launched Codex, described as its most capable AI coding agent yet, powered by codex-1 — a version of the o3 reasoning model optimized for software engineering. The agent runs in a sandboxed cloud environment preloaded with users' GitHub repositories and can handle multiple tasks simultaneously, taking between one and 30 minutes to write features, fix bugs, answer codebase questions, and propose pull requests. Codex is initially available to ChatGPT Pro, Enterprise, and Team subscribers at no extra cost, with rate limits and paid tiers planned for coming weeks. OpenAI's Agents Research Lead said the company envisions Codex eventually acting as 'virtual teammates' capable of completing autonomously what human engineers take hours or days to accomplish. Separately, OpenAI is also reportedly acquiring AI coding platform Windsurf for $3 billion, signaling a major push to dominate the fast-growing AI coding market.

title: Google DeepMind's AlphaEvolve Discovers Algorithms Better Than Humans
summary: Google DeepMind released AlphaEvolve, a Gemini-powered coding agent that evolves algorithms to solve complex math and real-world problems, already saving 0.7% of Google's global compute resources.
content: Google DeepMind unveiled AlphaEvolve, an evolutionary coding agent that uses the Gemini 2.0 LLM family to discover new algorithms and outperform existing human-written solutions. The system has Gemini Flash generate many candidate code solutions, scores each for accuracy and efficiency, then iteratively improves the best performers — a loop that can produce programs hundreds of lines long applicable to a wide variety of problems. In practical deployments, AlphaEvolve improved Google's data center scheduling software, recovering 0.7% of the company's total worldwide computing resources on an ongoing basis, and sped up Gemini model training by 1% through kernel optimization. Tested against over 50 open math problems spanning geometry, number theory, and Fourier analysis, AlphaEvolve matched the best known solutions in 75% of cases and found improvements in 20%. The tool also surpassed the specialized AlphaTensor on 4x4 matrix multiplication and found a way to reduce power consumption in Google's TPU chips, with researchers calling it a new essential tool for mathematics and computer science.

title: Anthropic Introduces Claude 4: Opus 4 Named World's Best Coding Model
summary: Anthropic launched Claude Opus 4 and Sonnet 4 with new extended thinking, parallel tool use, and memory capabilities — setting fresh benchmarks for coding agents and long-running AI workflows.
content: Anthropic introduced the Claude 4 model family — Claude Opus 4 and Claude Sonnet 4 — setting new benchmarks for coding, advanced reasoning, and AI agents. Claude Opus 4 leads SWE-bench Verified at 72.5% and Terminal-bench at 43.2%, and can sustain performance on complex tasks running continuously for several hours, dramatically expanding what AI agents can accomplish. Both models support extended thinking with tool use, allowing Claude to alternate between deep reasoning and web search mid-thought, while new memory capabilities let Opus 4 extract and save key facts to local files for continuity across long projects. Alongside the models, Anthropic announced Claude Code is now generally available with VS Code and JetBrains integrations, an extensible Claude Code SDK for building custom agents, and four new API capabilities: a code execution tool, MCP connector, Files API, and one-hour prompt caching. Leading AI coding platforms including Cursor, Replit, GitHub Copilot, and Cognition all praised Opus 4 as a meaningful leap forward in complex codebase understanding and multi-step execution.

title: U.S. House Passes 10-Year Moratorium on State AI Regulation
summary: The House passed the 'One Big Beautiful Bill' with a provision barring states from enforcing AI laws for a decade, drawing fierce opposition from 40 state attorneys general and policy experts.
content: The U.S. House of Representatives passed the 'One Big Beautiful Bill Act' by a razor-thin 215-214 vote, including a 10-year moratorium preventing any state or local government from enforcing laws or regulations covering AI models, AI systems, or automated decision systems. Critics warn the measure would block hundreds of already-enacted state laws protecting against AI-generated explicit content, election deepfakes, and algorithmic bias — with 40 state attorneys general signing a joint letter calling the moratorium 'sweeping and wholly destructive.' Legal experts say the provision likely violates the Senate's Byrd Rule, which restricts non-budgetary policy items from budget reconciliation bills, meaning it faces significant procedural hurdles in the Senate. Proponents argue that more than 1,000 AI-related state bills introduced in 2025 alone create compliance chaos for industry, and that federal preemption is needed to prevent a fragmented regulatory patchwork. Even if stripped from the reconciliation bill, Senator Ted Cruz has signaled plans to introduce a standalone 10-year AI moratorium through the Senate Commerce Committee, setting up a prolonged debate over federal versus state AI governance.

title: Microsoft Build 2025: Copilot Tuning and Multi-Agent Orchestration for the Enterprise
summary: Microsoft unveiled Copilot Tuning — a low-code tool for training AI on company data — and multi-agent orchestration allowing AI agents to collaborate as a coordinated team across enterprise workflows.
content: Microsoft unveiled several major AI advances at Build 2025, led by Copilot Tuning, a new low-code capability in Microsoft Copilot Studio that lets any organization fine-tune AI models on its own data, workflows, and processes without needing a dedicated data science team. Alongside it, multi-agent orchestration allows multiple AI agents to exchange data, divide work by each agent's expertise, and collaborate on complex tasks — for example, coordinating HR, IT, and marketing agents together to onboard a new employee. More than 230,000 organizations including 90% of the Fortune 500 already use Copilot Studio, and Microsoft projects 1.3 billion AI agents will be deployed across enterprises by 2028. Copilot Wave 2 moved to general availability including an updated Microsoft 365 Copilot app and Copilot Notebooks, while new Researcher and Analyst reasoning agents began rolling out globally. Developers also gained the Microsoft 365 Agents SDK, Agent2Agent (A2A) protocol support in the Teams AI Library, and Microsoft Entra Agent ID for managing agent identity and access using the same security infrastructure used for human users.

title: OpenAI Report: Enterprise AI Usage Grew 8x in a Year as Adoption Deepens
summary: OpenAI's State of Enterprise AI report reveals ChatGPT Enterprise usage grew 8x annually, with 75% of workers reporting faster or higher-quality output and 'frontier firms' pulling significantly ahead.
content: OpenAI published its first State of Enterprise AI report, drawing on real usage data from enterprise customers and a survey of 9,000 workers across nearly 100 companies, finding that AI adoption is accelerating in both breadth and depth. Weekly messages in ChatGPT Enterprise grew roughly 8x year-over-year, structured workflow usage increased 19x year-to-date, and average reasoning token consumption per organization surged approximately 320x over 12 months — indicating a shift from casual queries to deeply integrated, complex AI use. Workers report saving 40 to 60 minutes per day on average, with 75% saying AI has improved the speed or quality of their output, and 75% also reporting they can now complete new types of tasks they previously could not. A clear divide is emerging between 'frontier firms' — which send 2x more messages per seat and show deeper cross-team AI integration — and median organizations still in early experimentation. Technology, healthcare, and manufacturing are the fastest-growing sectors, while Australia, Brazil, the Netherlands, and France each saw enterprise customer bases grow more than 140% year-over-year.
